📬 What Callisto Mailer Actually Does
Callisto Mailer is a Symfony bundle. It stores email templates in a database table. Each template carries a code, a subject, an HTML body, and a locale. You write the templates in Twig. The bundle compiles them and wraps them in a layout. Then it sends them through the standard Symfony Mailer component.
The idea is simple. A marketing or support team can update an email subject or body without touching the codebase. Developers keep control over the required variables through a small contract system built into each template row.
Here is a fact worth stating plainly before we go further. This project is young. The GitHub repository shows a single release, tagged version 1.0.0, published on July 10, 2026. It carries one star and no forks. The package is not registered on Packagist under its declared name, callisto/callisto-mailer. The standard composer require command from the README will fail. You need a manual repository entry first, and we cover that step below.
None of this makes the code bad. The service class is clean and properly typed. Treat it as an early, low-adoption library, not as a mature tool with a community and a track record behind it.
